framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Build open-source scientific-computing tools the field runs on
paths.what_it_is: Write and maintain the interactive-computing and data-tooling infrastructure (notebooks, kernels, parallel-computing libraries, hosting infrastructure) that physicists and other scientists use daily, starting as a grad-student side project and growing it into foundational, widely-adopted software.
paths.why_it_signals: Three physics-trained researchers built IPython/Jupyter directly out of their physics graduate work — one from a particle-physics PhD, one from a physics PhD plus a physics professorship, one from an engineering-physics/plasma-physics background — and turned it into the computing substrate the entire scientific-Python ecosystem now runs on, showing that shipping open-source tooling is a buildable, learnable craft a new physics grad can start on day one, not a rare accident.

paths.path: Take a physics PhD into quantitative finance
paths.what_it_is: Apply the modeling and statistical-physics toolkit from a physics doctorate to build trading systems, risk models, or an entire quant fund.
paths.why_it_signals: Three physicists with physics PhDs (not math or economics) became foundational figures in quant finance — one effectively created the modern 'quant' role at a major bank, one co-founded one of the first automated trading firms, and one founded econophysics and built it into a major hedge fund — showing the physics-to-quant jump is a well-worn, repeatable route.

paths.path: Communicate physics to a broad public audience
paths.what_it_is: Build a durable, named public following (blog, podcast, or books) that explains physics to a general audience while remaining research-credentialed.
paths.why_it_signals: Three working physicists each built a distinct, large, long-running public platform (a research blog and YouTube channel, a philosophy-of-science blog and interview podcast, and bestselling books plus a science festival) on top of active physics careers, showing public communication is a buildable, repeated craft among standout physicists rather than incidental fame.
